item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to agree, with how this seems to be all about that whole "foundation is fascist, therefore not a single thing they do is good" trend that is slowly rising here, this is just peak that.

Lots of stuff is in this collapsible, all of which relies on the assumption that the authors intent is to follow that trend to an unhealthy degree.

If that is not the case, ignore it.
Look Zyn, I put the long text into a collapsible all by myself

I mean, this is not only ignoring all the anomalies that would literally end the world unless1 sacrifices are made to them, but also releasing millions of criminals into the world, if the demands are to be followed to the letter, without rehabilitation.
Unless this takes place in some kind of butterflies and rainbows kids-show universe where doing something for the right reasons absolves you and everyone else from the consequences of what you did to achieve those results, this d-class is now responsible for the end of about 5 universes and all the life in them.2
I am aware that the foundation is also not the best way to deal with it3 but they are aware of that and trying to improve.

Additionally, (headcanon stuff here) this ignores the compromise of releasing the D-class, meaning we had a good solution for the immorality of the program, which was not evil enough, so this article introduces ANOTHER flip in evil-ness in order for this ending to be "righteous" enough to still be able to stand valiantly on the pile of corpses from those universes, proclaiming "We did it! The evil thing is no more!"

… I just got why this is making me so mad.
This is reminiscent of someone finding out a person made a racist tweet, finding out that they apologized and changed for the better, then still going after them and ruining their life. 10 years after that apology.

With that, since neither the ideology nor the article agrees with me, both for the reasons outlined above, this is a strong -1 from me. Honestly, if this had a different ending with less of that, no offense intended, "the “screw the man” energy on offer here" this would maybe be a +0.2 for me overall.

Footnotes
1. living, real, soul having
2. If it DID take place in such a universe, that would also absolve the foundation.
3. I read a couple stories from the "no foundation" hub or what it was called

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very fun, enjoy the “screw the man” energy on offer here

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm… confused by what the D-Class in question thinks it'll accomplish by forcing the Foundation to stop the D-Class program. The amount of SCPs and anomalies that require D-Class personnel is probably at least a thousand- and probably a third of them could end the world without said D-Class intervention.

I suppose you could chalk that up to self-righteousness, and I'll grudgingly give said D-Class personnel an iota of my respect for being able to stick to his ideals even knowing that they'll probably result in the end of the world, or at the minimum, another Occult War with millions of casualties. And even the disbandment of the D-Class program does nothing, considering now you've got all those free, juicy criminals just waiting there for your rival organization to snatch up for their own experiments. Thanks for nothing, D-2231. You've accomplished nothing beyond satisfying your own ego.

And then there's the fact that the Foundation caved far too easily. Sure, D-2231 could release all the information in one big bang… and then what? It's played its trump card, and now it has nothing else to play. And it's not like the Foundation couldn't survive the aftermath- the Veil might be irrevocably torn, but they could probably bounce back with all the resources at their disposal. And after that… then what? D-2231 played its only card, and now it's got nothing left- sure, it can continue to broadcast information, but after the first few months of fallout, it's got nothing left, because it's already played all its cards in one fell swoop.

Dumb, self-righteous D-Class personnel was bad enough, but the response of the O5 Council made it worse. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the primordial truth is that because D2251 is caught up with his own self-righteousness he's willing to end the world, because if he can see the truth then he knows, unequivocally and without doubt, there are things that demand the lives and deaths of D-Class to not annihilate existence.
Sort of explains how he was the sort of person who wound up a permanent D-Class, doesn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just all over the place, and not in a good way. The addendums start as quasi-historical fiction and ends up destroying the Foundation, and the transition between those two stories is as natural as a plastic palm tree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback! I've adjusted that a bit to make things a little clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor crit.

+ show block
– hide block

This sentence threw me off

Following this interview, all buildings, equipment and personnel associated with the SCP Foundation outside SCP-5255 were destroyed via spontaneous combustion.

because my first thought was it meant "universally". I think it needs some kind of "nearby" clarification?
Now the foundation can final use clones for their experiment and I know they can make them seen that they have SCP 2000 for goodness sake, but I really like this skip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is some "fuck the system" goodness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you went hogwild and really emphasized on the 'wild'. love these same as always. +1 : )

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Going hog-wild on this one my friends

PLEASE DON'T SUE TANHONY
